item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - Wikiwalk
item.content:encoded: That's fair, the connection between them was rather tenuous. This was one I could have left as standalone, and I wasn't able to find many better crosslinks.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - Wikiwalk
item.content:encoded: Hm. While I'm open to the idea of it, I feel a little hesitant to include that crosslink in there. I can see the similarities between the two articles, but I think that it would be better to just leave SCP-3407 the way it is now. The tone of the two pieces is on two separate spectrums in my opinion.

item.title: Junior Staff Post - Wikiwalk
item.content:encoded:
The WikiWalk Team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

Edits denied by author, see below.

+ show block
– hide block

Reading this, I was reminded of SCP-205 for similar anomalies and general plot. I feel a crosslink would be appropriate.

Changing this:

SCP-3407 will always project its own film whether or not a reel is placed within its feed spool, though it has been shown to produce lower quality projections when a reel is within SCP-3407. SCP-3407 has also exhibited the ability to activate without any form of electricity or manual operation. Attempts to activate SCP-3407 outside of its scheduled projection have all been met with failure.

To this:

SCP-3407 will always project its own film whether or not a reel is placed within its feed spool, though it has been shown to produce lower quality projections when a reel is within SCP-3407. SCP-3407 has also exhibited the ability to activate without any form of electricity or manual operation. Attempts to activate SCP-3407 outside of its scheduled projection have all been met with failure. Due to the similarity in function, an investigation of a connection between SCP-3407 and SCP-205 is underway.

If anyone has any other ideas, feel free to suggest them below.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have re-hosted the article's image on-site with the author's permission. Credits for the image are in his original post, but I'll include it here just in case as well.
Wikimedia Commons

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cool to find an author that focuses their articles primarily on emotionally harrowing SCPs. You obviously have the creative talent for it. Enthusiastic +1 from me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I loved this when you first started drafting it a long time ago. It's been a delight to watch this grow from conception to post. Needless to say, I have downvoted with extreme prejudice.1
Keep up the excellent work, I look forward to seeing more of your stuff.

Footnotes
1. I am of course, completely serious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow, I'm honestly really flattered to have read that. This is probably the first time anyone's really said something like that about a story I wrote.

Your message honestly made me smile, I'm glad you enjoyed the read. :)

item.title: Re: Author's note
item.content:encoded:
For the record, you can link usernames with this syntax:

+ show block
– hide block
 [[*user not_a_seagull]]

which will result in:

not_a_seagull

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really liked this in draft form, really like it here. Hearty +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dark and unsettling, and a take at the racism of the era. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is incredibly underrated. One of the most visceral depictions of the sorrow of institutional racism that I've ever read in fiction. It reminds me of learning about irl cases like Emmet Till. Just utterly soul destroying.

Bravo, and an enthusiastic +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Checking back here, I'm happy with the reception this has gotten.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An interesting mix of emotional and unsettling. A flavor I don't think I see that often! +1 from me here.

(also, small thing to fix, I think it would be "illuminates" and not "luminates"?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm glad you enjoyed it! During one of the early drafts, I wrote the age of the girl in projection 4. Someone pointed out to me, however, that the foundation wouldn't know the exact age of the girl just looking at her, so I replaced it with her height instead. I didn't want the foundation to look like they're making assumptions, so I stuck with her height to allow the girl's age to be up to reader's interpretation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really enjoyed this, but one note:

estimated to be close to 0.64 meters

It seems odd to estimate the height of a child rather than the age. The height is mostly irrelevant except for estimating age.

item.title: Author's note
item.content:encoded:
This is the second SCP that I've ever written, hopefully, this does well on the main list. Special thanks to Uncle Nicolini, Zyn, PeppersGhost and Oystershell for helping me during the drafting process!

My main goal for this SCP was to be a short story that tugs at your heartstrings while still having an underlying horror/mystery element to it.

Image credit: Wikimedia Commons

Enjoyed this SCP? Click here to check out more of my work on my Author Page!
